## [03:00 - 06:02]

the offer there is slightly different and you're probably going to be sending them email at a different frequency. Maybe they're getting an email once a week over here or every other week or two times a week, but once you get them into a launch, you're probably going to be emailing them a lot more. You'll be emailing for each piece of pre-launch content. You'll probably be mailing them in between each piece of pre-launch content. Once you open cart here, and you have your cart open that five to seven days, you're going to be emailing them probably two times on that first day and then you're going to be emailing them each day and then in the closing day, you're probably going to be emailing them two or even three times. So if you add up all the email you're going to send them during the launch, you're probably going to send them in the order of at least 10, 12, maybe 15 emails. Now, a lot of people are like, well, won't that make them mad at me? Won't they unsubscribe from you? And the answer is no, they won't if they're interested in getting the material. And the way that you know that they're interested is because they went through this process of joining your new launch list. ## [06:02 - 08:04]

This can vary dramatically. I've seen it where it was like 80% of the people went over. But typically, 10 or 20% of your list, joining your launch list, you're doing pretty good. And these people are gonna be pure goal because they are very much interested in this. And the tests I've shown, just getting people to go through this process, these people are more responsive, just having gone through this squeeze page process, than they would have been if those same 2,000 people were over on this list. So it rejuvenates them. It brings that recency again, by going through this process of raising their hand and saying, yes, they've gone through that recency process and now they're that much warmer. And frankly, I see some serious, crazy numbers that these people are worth once they go through that process. Now, is it possible to do a launch, just to not to bother with this, and just to start sending out your prelaunch content over here to your main list? Absolutely, you can. If this launch is going to be for a product that is very much in alignment to what they opted in for. In other words, if they opted in for how to grow tomatoes and then your launch is going to be, how to grow heirloom tomatoes, very, very similar match, you could just go ahead and send your entire list the entire launch. You could, if whatever the launch is going to be for is directly in alignment with what they originally joined your list for, you can do that. Especially if you've never done any kind of a launch before, maybe you do run your entire list through the entire sequence. But in general, I prefer to run people through a squeeze page and start a new launch list. And like I said, those launch lists, it's like this warm fuzzy blanket because they are so powerful once people go through that process and join your launch list.
